description: Performing long, blocking, or allocating work inside an interrupt handler, delaying or dropping higher-priority interrupts and missing deadlines.
body: Failure mode: an ISR does heavy processing, logging, a blocking driver call, or malloc, so it runs for milliseconds; higher-priority interrupts are delayed or lost and real-time deadlines slip. Detection signals: measured ISR duration far exceeds budget; dropped interrupts or input under load; system jitter that disappears when the load drops. Do not do blocking or unbounded work in an ISR. Safer fallback: in the ISR only clear the source and post a semaphore/queue to a worker task, move processing to the base level, and verify worst-case ISR duration on a logic analyzer fits the latency budget.
